name: activity_stream
exclusionReason: Runtime audit stream entries are not desired-state configuration objects.

fields.name: action_node
fields.description: The cluster node the activity took place on.

fields.name: changes
fields.description: A summary of the new and changed values when an object is created, updated, or deleted

fields.name: object1
fields.description: For create, update, and delete events this is the object type that was affected. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type associated or disassociated with object2.

fields.name: object2
fields.description: Unpopulated for create, update, and delete events. For associate and disassociate events this is the object type that object1 is being associated with.

fields.name: object_association
fields.description: When present, shows the field name of the role or relationship that changed.

fields.name: object_type
fields.description: When present, shows the model on which the role or relationship was defined.

fields.name: operation
fields.description:
The action taken with respect to the given object(s).

* `create` - Entity Created
* `update` - Entity Updated
* `delete` - Entity Deleted
* `associate` - Entity Associated with another Entity
* `disassociate` - Entity was Disassociated with another Entity

name: ad_hoc_commands
exclusionReason: Ad-hoc command runs are runtime execution records.

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: job_type
fields.description:
* `run` - Run
* `check` - Check

fields.name: module_name
fields.description:
* `command` - command
* `shell` - shell
* `yum` - yum
* `apt` - apt
* `apt_key` - apt_key
* `apt_repository` - apt_repository
* `apt_rpm` - apt_rpm
* `service` - service
* `group` - group
* `user` - user
* `mount` - mount
* `ping` - ping
* `selinux` - selinux
* `setup` - setup
* `win_ping` - win_ping
* `win_service` - win_service
* `win_updates` - win_updates
* `win_group` - win_group
* `win_user` - win_user

fields.name: verbosity
fields.description:
* `0` - 0 (Normal)
* `1` - 1 (Verbose)
* `2` - 2 (More Verbose)
* `3` - 3 (Debug)
* `4` - 4 (Connection Debug)
* `5` - 5 (WinRM Debug)

fields.name: limit
fields.description: The limit to restrict the returned hosts for the related auto-created inventory source, special to constructed inventory.

fields.name: opa_query_path
fields.description: The query path for the OPA policy to evaluate prior to job execution. The query path should be formatted as package/rule.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: Organization containing this inventory.

fields.name: prevent_instance_group_fallback
fields.description: If enabled, the inventory will prevent adding any organization instance groups to the list of preferred instances groups to run associated job templates on.If this setting is enabled and you provided an empty list, the global instance groups will be applied.

fields.name: source_vars
fields.description: The source_vars for the related auto-created inventory source, special to constructed inventory.

fields.name: update_cache_timeout
fields.description: The cache timeout for the related auto-created inventory source, special to constructed inventory

fields.name: variables
fields.description: Inventory variables in JSON or YAML format.

fields.name: verbosity
fields.description: The verbosity level for the related auto-created inventory source, special to constructed inventory

fields.name: injectors
fields.description: Enter injectors using either JSON or YAML syntax. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.

fields.name: inputs
fields.description: Enter inputs using either JSON or YAML syntax. Refer to the documentation for example syntax.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: * `cloud` - Cloud\n* `net` - Network

fields.name: credential_type
fields.description: Specify the type of credential you want to create. Refer to the documentation for details on each type.

fields.name: inputs
fields.description: Credential inputs are treated as write-only secret payloads.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: Inherit permissions from organization roles. If provided on creation, do not give either user or team.

fields.name: team
fields.description: Write-only field used to add team to owner role. If provided, do not give either user or organization. Only valid for creation.

fields.name: user
fields.description: Write-only field used to add user to owner role. If provided, do not give either team or organization. Only valid for creation.

fields.name: image
fields.description: The full image location, including the container registry, image name, and version tag.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: The organization used to determine access to this execution environment.

fields.name: pull
fields.description:
Pull image before running?

* `always` - Always pull container before running.
* `missing` - Only pull the image if not present before running.
* `never` - Never pull container before running.

fields.name: variables
fields.description: Group variables in JSON or YAML format.

name: host_metrics
exclusionReason: Host metrics are runtime telemetry and not managed desired-state configuration.

fields.name: automated_counter
fields.description: How many times was the host automated

fields.name: deleted
fields.description: Boolean flag saying whether the host is deleted and therefore not counted into the subscription consumption

fields.name: deleted_counter
fields.description: How many times was the host deleted

fields.name: first_automation
fields.description: When the host was first automated against

fields.name: last_automation
fields.description: When the host was last automated against

fields.name: last_deleted
fields.description: When the host was last deleted

fields.name: used_in_inventories
fields.description: How many inventories contain this host

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Is this host online and available for running jobs?

fields.name: instance_id
fields.description: The value used by the remote inventory source to uniquely identify the host

fields.name: variables
fields.description: Host variables in JSON or YAML format.

fields.name: is_container_group
fields.description: Indicates whether instances in this group are containerized.Containerized groups have a designated Openshift or Kubernetes cluster.

fields.name: max_concurrent_jobs
fields.description: Maximum number of concurrent jobs to run on a group. When set to zero, no maximum is enforced.

fields.name: max_forks
fields.description: Maximum number of forks to execute concurrently on a group. When set to zero, no maximum is enforced.

fields.name: policy_instance_list
fields.description: List of exact-match Instances that will be assigned to this group

fields.name: policy_instance_minimum
fields.description: Static minimum number of Instances that will be automatically assign to this group when new instances come online.

fields.name: policy_instance_percentage
fields.description: Minimum percentage of all instances that will be automatically assigned to this group when new instances come online.

name: instances
exclusionReason: AWX instances represent runtime cluster topology and are not managed by this provider.

fields.name: node_state
fields.description:
Indicates the current life cycle stage of this instance.

* `provisioning` - Provisioning
* `provision-fail` - Provisioning Failure
* `installed` - Installed
* `ready` - Ready
* `unavailable` - Unavailable
* `deprovisioning` - De-provisioning
* `deprovision-fail` - De-provisioning Failure

fields.name: node_type
fields.description:
Role that this node plays in the mesh.

* `control` - Control plane node
* `execution` - Execution plane node
* `hybrid` - Controller and execution
* `hop` - Message-passing node, no execution capability

fields.name: peers
fields.description: Primary keys of receptor addresses to peer to.

fields.name: host_filter
fields.description: Filter that will be applied to the hosts of this inventory.

fields.name: kind
fields.description:
Kind of inventory being represented.

* `` - Hosts have a direct link to this inventory.
* `smart` - Hosts for inventory generated using the host_filter property.
* `constructed` - Parse list of source inventories with the constructed inventory plugin.

fields.name: opa_query_path
fields.description: The query path for the OPA policy to evaluate prior to job execution. The query path should be formatted as package/rule.

fields.name: organization
fields.description: Organization containing this inventory.

fields.name: prevent_instance_group_fallback
fields.description: If enabled, the inventory will prevent adding any organization instance groups to the list of preferred instances groups to run associated job templates on.If this setting is enabled and you provided an empty list, the global instance groups will be applied.

fields.name: variables
fields.description: Inventory variables in JSON or YAML format.

fields.name: credential
fields.description: Cloud credential to use for inventory updates.

fields.name: enabled_value
fields.description: Only used when enabled_var is set. Value when the host is considered enabled. For example if enabled_var="status.power_state"and enabled_value="powered_on" with host variables:{   "status": {     "power_state": "powered_on",     "created": "2020-08-04T18:13:04+00:00",     "healthy": true    },    "name": "foobar",    "ip_address": "192.168.2.1"}The host would be marked enabled. If power_state where any value other than powered_on then the host would be disabled when imported. If the key is not found then the host will be enabled

fields.name: enabled_var
fields.description: Retrieve the enabled state from the given dict of host variables. The enabled variable may be specified as "foo.bar", in which case the lookup will traverse into nested dicts, equivalent to: from_dict.get("foo", {}).get("bar", default)

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: host_filter
fields.description: This field is deprecated and will be removed in a future release. Regex where only matching hosts will be imported.

fields.name: limit
fields.description: Enter host, group or pattern match

fields.name: overwrite
fields.description: Overwrite local groups and hosts from remote inventory source.

fields.name: overwrite_vars
fields.description: Overwrite local variables from remote inventory source.

fields.name: scm_branch
fields.description: Inventory source SCM branch. Project default used if blank. Only allowed if project allow_override field is set to true.

If power_state where any value other than powered_on then the host would be disabled when imported. If the key is not found then the host will be enabled

fields.description: Websocket path.

fields.name: content_type
fields.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

fields.name: description
fields.description: A description of this role.

fields.name: name
fields.description: The name of this role.

fields.name: object_ansible_id
fields.description: The resource id of the object this role applies to. An alternative to the object_id field.

fields.name: object_id
fields.description: The primary key of the object this assignment applies to; null value indicates system-wide assignment.

fields.name: role_definition
fields.description: The role definition which defines permissions conveyed by this assignment.

fields.name: team_ansible_id
fields.description: The resource ID of the team who will receive permissions from this assignment. An alternative to team field.

fields.name: content_type
fields.description: String to use for references to this type from other models in the API.

fields.name: created
fields.description: The date/time this resource was created.

fields.name: created_by
fields.description: The user who created this resource.

fields.name: object_ansible_id
fields.description: The resource id of the object this role applies to. An alternative to the object_id field.

fields.name: object_id
fields.description: The primary key of the object this assignment applies to; null value indicates system-wide assignment.

fields.name: role_definition
fields.description: The role definition which defines permissions conveyed by this assignment.

fields.name: user_ansible_id
fields.description: The resource ID of the user who will receive permissions from this assignment. An alternative to user field.

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Enables processing of this schedule.

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: inventory
fields.description: Inventory applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for inventory

fields.name: job_type
fields.description:
* `run` - Run
* `check` - Check

fields.name: rrule
fields.description: A value representing the schedules iCal recurrence rule.

fields.name: verbosity
fields.description:
* `0` - 0 (Normal)
* `1` - 1 (Verbose)
* `2` - 2 (More Verbose)
* `3` - 3 (Debug)
* `4` - 4 (Connection Debug)
* `5` - 5 (WinRM Debug)

fields.name: ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED
fields.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream.

fields.name: ACTIVITY_STREAM_ENABLED_FOR_INVENTORY_SYNC
fields.description: Enable capturing activity for the activity stream when running inventory sync.

fields.name: AD_HOC_COMMANDS
fields.description: List of modules allowed to be used by ad-hoc jobs.

fields.name: ALLOW_JINJA_IN_EXTRA_VARS
fields.description:
Ansible allows variable substitution via the Jinja2 templating language for --extra-vars. This poses a potential security risk where users with the ability to specify extra vars at job launch time can use Jinja2 templates to run arbitrary Python.  It is recommended that this value be set to "template" or "never".

* `always` - Always
* `never` - Never
* `template` - Only On Job Template Definitions

fields.name: ALLOW_METRICS_FOR_ANONYMOUS_USERS
fields.description: If true, anonymous users are allowed to poll metrics.

fields.name: ANSIBLE_FACT_CACHE_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Maximum time, in seconds, that stored Ansible facts are considered valid since the last time they were modified. Only valid, non-stale, facts will be accessible by a playbook. Note, this does not influence the deletion of ansible_facts from the database. Use a value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed.

fields.name: API_400_ERROR_LOG_FORMAT
fields.description:
The format of logged messages when an API 4XX error occurs, the following variables will be substituted: 
status_code - The HTTP status code of the error
user_name - The user name attempting to use the API
url_path - The URL path to the API endpoint called
remote_addr - The remote address seen for the user
error - The error set by the api endpoint
Variables need to be in the format {<variable name>}.

fields.name: AUTH_BASIC_ENABLED
fields.description: Enable HTTP Basic Auth for the API Browser.

fields.name: AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_GATHER_INTERVAL
fields.description: Interval (in seconds) between data gathering.

fields.name: AUTOMATION_ANALYTICS_URL
fields.description: This setting is used to to configure the upload URL for data collection for Automation Analytics.

fields.name: AWX_ANSIBLE_CALLBACK_PLUGINS
fields.description: List of paths to search for extra callback plugins to be used when running jobs. Enter one path per line.

fields.name: AWX_CLEANUP_PATHS
fields.description: Enable or Disable TMP Dir cleanup

fields.name: AWX_COLLECTIONS_ENABLED
fields.description: Allows collections to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.

fields.name: AWX_ISOLATION_BASE_PATH
fields.description: The directory in which the service will create new temporary directories for job execution and isolation (such as credential files).

fields.name: AWX_ISOLATION_SHOW_PATHS
fields.description: List of paths that would otherwise be hidden to expose to isolated jobs. Enter one path per line. Volumes will be mounted from the execution node to the container. The supported format is HOST-DIR[:CONTAINER-DIR[:OPTIONS]].

fields.name: AWX_MOUNT_ISOLATED_PATHS_ON_K8S
fields.description: Expose paths via hostPath for the Pods created by a Container Group. HostPath volumes present many security risks, and it is a best practice to avoid the use of HostPaths when possible.

fields.name: AWX_REQUEST_PROFILE
fields.description: Debug web request python timing

fields.name: AWX_ROLES_ENABLED
fields.description: Allows roles to be dynamically downloaded from a requirements.yml file for SCM projects.

fields.name: AWX_RUNNER_KEEPALIVE_SECONDS
fields.description: Only applies to jobs running in a Container Group. If not 0, send a message every so-many seconds to keep connection open.

fields.name: AWX_SHOW_PLAYBOOK_LINKS
fields.description: Follow symbolic links when scanning for playbooks. Be aware that setting this to True can lead to infinite recursion if a link points to a parent directory of itself.

fields.name: AWX_TASK_ENV
fields.description: Additional environment variables set for playbook runs, inventory updates, project updates, and notification sending.

fields.name: BULK_HOST_MAX_CREATE
fields.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be created in a single bulk action

fields.name: BULK_HOST_MAX_DELETE
fields.description: Max number of hosts to allow to be deleted in a single bulk action

fields.name: BULK_JOB_MAX_LAUNCH
fields.description: Max jobs to allow bulk jobs to launch

fields.name: CSRF_TRUSTED_ORIGINS
fields.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the schema://addresses from which the service should trust Origin header values.

fields.name: CUSTOM_LOGIN_INFO
fields.description: If needed, you can add specific information (such as a legal notice or a disclaimer) to a text box in the login modal using this setting. Any content added must be in plain text or an HTML fragment, as other markup languages are not supported.

fields.name: CUSTOM_LOGO
fields.description: To set up a custom logo, provide a file that you create. For the custom logo to look its best, use a .png file with a transparent background. GIF, PNG and JPEG formats are supported.

fields.name: CUSTOM_VENV_PATHS
fields.description: Paths where Tower will look for custom virtual environments (in addition to /var/lib/awx/venv/). Enter one path per line.

fields.name: DEFAULT_CONTAINER_RUN_OPTIONS
fields.description: List of options to pass to podman run example: ['--network', 'slirp4netns:enable_ipv6=true', '--log-level', 'debug']

fields.name: DEFAULT_EXECUTION_ENVIRONMENT
fields.description: The Execution Environment to be used when one has not been configured for a job template.

fields.name: DEFAULT_INVENTORY_UPDATE_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow inventory updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual inventory source will override this.

fields.name: DEFAULT_JOB_IDLE_TIMEOUT
fields.description: If no output is detected from ansible in this number of seconds the execution will be terminated. Use value of 0 to indicate that no idle timeout should be imposed.

fields.name: DEFAULT_JOB_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow jobs to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual job template will override this.

fields.name: DEFAULT_PROJECT_UPDATE_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Maximum time in seconds to allow project updates to run. Use value of 0 to indicate that no timeout should be imposed. A timeout set on an individual project will override this.

fields.name: DISABLE_LOCAL_AUTH
fields.description: Controls whether users are prevented from using the built-in authentication system.

fields.name: EVENT_STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY
fields.description: Maximum Size of Standard Output in bytes to display for a single job or ad hoc command event. `stdout` will end with `…` when truncated.

fields.name: GALAXY_IGNORE_CERTS
fields.description: If set to true, certificate validation will not be done when installing content from any Galaxy server.

fields.name: GALAXY_TASK_ENV
fields.description: Additional environment variables set for invocations of ansible-galaxy within project updates. Useful if you must use a proxy server for ansible-galaxy but not git.

fields.name: INSIGHTS_TRACKING_STATE
fields.description: Enables the service to gather data on automation and send it to Automation Analytics.

fields.name: LOGIN_REDIRECT_OVERRIDE
fields.description: URL to which unauthorized users will be redirected to log in.  If blank, users will be sent to the login page.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_MAX_DISK_USAGE_GB
fields.description: Amount of data to store (in gigabytes) if an rsyslog action takes time to process an incoming message (defaults to 1). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.maxdiskspace setting on the action (e.g. omhttp). It stores files in the directory specified by LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_ACTION_QUEUE_SIZE
fields.description: Defines how large the rsyslog action queue can grow in number of messages stored. This can have an impact on memory utilization. When the queue reaches 75% of this number, the queue will start writing to disk (queue.highWatermark in rsyslog). When it reaches 90%, NOTICE, INFO, and DEBUG messages will start to be discarded (queue.discardMark with queue.discardSeverity=5).

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_ENABLED
fields.description: Enable sending logs to external log aggregator.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_HOST
fields.description: Hostname/IP where external logs will be sent to.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_INDIVIDUAL_FACTS
fields.description: If set, system tracking facts will be sent for each package, service, or other item found in a scan, allowing for greater search query granularity. If unset, facts will be sent as a single dictionary, allowing for greater efficiency in fact processing.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_LEVEL
fields.description:
Level threshold used by log handler. Severities from lowest to highest are DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR, CRITICAL. Messages less severe than the threshold will be ignored by log handler. (messages under category awx.anlytics ignore this setting)

* `DEBUG` - DEBUG
* `INFO` - INFO
* `WARNING` - WARNING
* `ERROR` - ERROR
* `CRITICAL` - CRITICAL

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_LOGGERS
fields.description:
List of loggers that will send HTTP logs to the collector, these can include any or all of: 
awx - service logs
activity_stream - activity stream records
job_events - callback data from Ansible job events
system_tracking - facts gathered from scan jobs
broadcast_websocket - errors pertaining to websockets broadcast metrics
job_lifecycle - logs related to processing of a job

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_MAX_DISK_USAGE_PATH
fields.description: Location to persist logs that should be retried after an outage of the external log aggregator (defaults to /var/lib/awx). Equivalent to the rsyslogd queue.spoolDirectory setting.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_PASSWORD
fields.description: Password or authentication token for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_PORT
fields.description: Port on Logging Aggregator to send logs to (if required and not provided in Logging Aggregator).

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL
fields.description:
Protocol used to communicate with log aggregator.  HTTPS/HTTP assumes HTTPS unless http:// is explicitly used in the Logging Aggregator hostname.

* `https` - HTTPS/HTTP
* `tcp` - TCP
* `udp` - UDP

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_RSYSLOGD_DEBUG
fields.description: Enabled high verbosity debugging for rsyslogd.  Useful for debugging connection issues for external log aggregation.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_TCP_TIMEOUT
fields.description: Number of seconds for a TCP connection to external log aggregator to timeout. Applies to HTTPS and TCP log aggregator protocols.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_TOWER_UUID
fields.description: Useful to uniquely identify instances.

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_TYPE
fields.description:
Format messages for the chosen log aggregator.

* `logstash` - logstash
* `splunk` - splunk
* `loggly` - loggly
* `sumologic` - sumologic
* `other` - other

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_USERNAME
fields.description: Username for external log aggregator (if required; HTTP/s only).

fields.name: LOG_AGGREGATOR_VERIFY_CERT
fields.description: Flag to control enable/disable of certificate verification when LOG_AGGREGATOR_PROTOCOL is "https". If enabled, the log handler will verify certificate sent by external log aggregator before establishing connection.

fields.name: MANAGE_ORGANIZATION_AUTH
fields.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin has the privileges to create and manage users and teams.

fields.name: MAX_FORKS
fields.description: Saving a Job Template with more than this number of forks will result in an error. When set to 0, no limit is applied.

fields.name: MAX_UI_JOB_EVENTS
fields.description: Maximum number of job events for the UI to retrieve within a single request.

fields.name: MAX_WEBSOCKET_EVENT_RATE
fields.description: Maximum number of messages to update the UI live job output with per second. Value of 0 means no limit.

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_CA_CERT
fields.description: The content of the CA certificate for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_CERT
fields.description: The content of the client certificate file for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_CLIENT_KEY
fields.description: The content of the client key for mTLS authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Certificate".

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS
fields.description: Optional custom headers included in requests to the OPA server. Defaults to empty dictionary ({}).

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_TOKEN
fields.description: The token for authentication to the OPA server. Required when OPA_AUTH_TYPE is "Token". If an authorization header is defined in OPA_AUTH_CUSTOM_HEADERS, it will be overridden by OPA_AUTH_TOKEN.

fields.name: OPA_AUTH_TYPE
fields.description:
The authentication type that will be used to connect to the OPA server: "None", "Token", or "Certificate".

* `None` - None
* `Token` - Token
* `Certificate` - Certificate

fields.name: OPA_HOST
fields.description: The hostname used to connect to the OPA server. If empty, policy enforcement will be disabled.

fields.name: OPA_PORT
fields.description: The port used to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to 8181.

fields.name: OPA_REQUEST_RETRIES
fields.description: The number of retry attempts for connecting to the OPA server. Default is 2.

fields.name: OPA_REQUEST_TIMEOUT
fields.description: The number of seconds after which the connection to the OPA server will time out. Defaults to 1.5 seconds.

fields.name: OPA_SSL
fields.description: Enable or disable the use of SSL to connect to the OPA server. Defaults to false.

fields.name: ORG_ADMINS_CAN_SEE_ALL_USERS
fields.description: Controls whether any Organization Admin can view all users and teams, even those not associated with their Organization.

fields.name: PROJECT_UPDATE_VVV
fields.description: Adds the CLI -vvv flag to ansible-playbook runs of project_update.yml used for project updates.

fields.name: PROXY_IP_ALLOWED_LIST
fields.description: If the service is behind a reverse proxy/load balancer, use this setting to configure the proxy IP addresses from which the service should trust custom REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS header values. If this setting is an empty list (the default), the headers specified by REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS will be trusted unconditionally')

fields.name: RECEPTOR_KEEP_WORK_ON_ERROR
fields.description: Prevent receptor work from being released on when error is detected

fields.name: RECEPTOR_RELEASE_WORK
fields.description: Release receptor work

fields.name: REDHAT_PASSWORD
fields.description: Client secret used to send data to Automation Analytics

fields.name: REDHAT_USERNAME
fields.description: Client ID used to send data to Automation Analytics

fields.name: REMOTE_HOST_HEADERS
fields.description: HTTP headers and meta keys to search to determine remote host name or IP. Add additional items to this list, such as "HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR", if behind a reverse proxy. See the "Proxy Support" section of the AAP Installation guide for more details.

fields.name: SCHEDULE_MAX_JOBS
fields.description: Maximum number of the same job template that can be waiting to run when launching from a schedule before no more are created.

fields.name: SESSIONS_PER_USER
fields.description: Maximum number of simultaneous logged in sessions a user may have. To disable enter -1.

fields.name: SESSION_COOKIE_AGE
fields.description: Number of seconds that a user is inactive before they will need to login again.

fields.name: STDOUT_MAX_BYTES_DISPLAY
fields.description: Maximum Size of Standard Output in bytes to display before requiring the output be downloaded.

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS_CLIENT_ID
fields.description: Client ID used to retrieve subscription and content information

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS_CLIENT_SECRET
fields.description: Client secret used to retrieve subscription and content information

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS_PASSWORD
fields.description: Password used to retrieve subscription and content information

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTIONS_USERNAME
fields.description: Username used to retrieve subscription and content information

fields.name: SUBSCRIPTION_USAGE_MODEL
fields.description:
* `` - No subscription. Deletion of host_metrics will not be considered for purposes of managed host counting
* `unique_managed_hosts` - Usage based on unique managed nodes in a large historical time frame and delete functionality for no longer used managed nodes

fields.name: TOWER_URL_BASE
fields.description: This setting is used by services like notifications to render a valid url to the service.

fields.name: UI_LIVE_UPDATES_ENABLED
fields.description: If disabled, the page will not refresh when events are received. Reloading the page will be required to get the latest details.

name: system_job_templates
exclusionReason: System job templates are runtime/operator endpoints not in managed desired-state scope.

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: job_type
fields.description:
* `cleanup_jobs` - Remove jobs older than a certain number of days
* `cleanup_activitystream` - Remove activity stream entries older than a certain number of days
* `cleanup_sessions` - Removes expired browser sessions from the database

fields.name: status
fields.description:
* `new` - New
* `pending` - Pending
* `waiting` - Waiting
* `running` - Running
* `successful` - Successful
* `failed` - Failed
* `error` - Error
* `canceled` - Canceled
* `never updated` - Never Updated
* `ok` - OK
* `missing` - Missing
* `none` - No External Source
* `updating` - Updating

name: system_jobs
exclusionReason: System jobs are runtime execution records.

fields.name: canceled_on
fields.description: The date and time when the cancel request was sent.

fields.name: elapsed
fields.description: Elapsed time in seconds that the job ran.

fields.name: event_processing_finished
fields.description: Indicates whether all of the events generated by this unified job have been saved to the database.

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: execution_node
fields.description: The node the job executed on.

fields.name: finished
fields.description: The date and time the job finished execution.

fields.name: job_explanation
fields.description: A status field to indicate the state of the job if it wasn't able to run and capture stdout

fields.name: job_type
fields.description:
* `cleanup_jobs` - Remove jobs older than a certain number of days
* `cleanup_activitystream` - Remove activity stream entries older than a certain number of days
* `cleanup_sessions` - Removes expired browser sessions from the database

fields.name: launch_type
fields.description:
* `manual` - Manual
* `relaunch` - Relaunch
* `callback` - Callback
* `scheduled` - Scheduled
* `dependency` - Dependency
* `workflow` - Workflow
* `webhook` - Webhook
* `sync` - Sync
* `scm` - SCM Update

fields.name: started
fields.description: The date and time the job was queued for starting.

fields.name: status
fields.description:
* `new` - New
* `pending` - Pending
* `waiting` - Waiting
* `running` - Running
* `successful` - Successful
* `failed` - Failed
* `error` - Error
* `canceled` - Canceled

fields.name: work_unit_id
fields.description: The Receptor work unit ID associated with this job.

fields.name: is_superuser
fields.description: Designates that this user has all permissions without explicitly assigning them.

fields.name: password
fields.description: Field used to change the password.

fields.name: username
fields.description: Required. 150 characters or fewer. Letters, digits and @/./+/-/_ only.

fields.name: timed_out
fields.description: Shows when an approval node (with a timeout assigned to it) has timed out.

fields.name: work_unit_id

name: workflow_job_nodes
exclusionReason: Workflow job nodes are runtime execution records.

fields.name: all_parents_must_converge
fields.description: If enabled then the node will only run if all of the parent nodes have met the criteria to reach this node

fields.name: do_not_run
fields.description: Indicates that a job will not be created when True. Workflow runtime semantics will mark this True if the node is in a path that will decidedly not be ran. A value of False means the node may not run.

fields.name: execution_environment
fields.description: The container image to be used for execution.

fields.name: identifier
fields.description: An identifier coresponding to the workflow job template node that this node was created from.

fields.name: inventory
fields.description: Inventory applied as a prompt, assuming job template prompts for inventory

fields.description:
* `run` - Run
* `check` - Check

fields.name: verbosity

* `0` - 0 (Normal)
* `1` - 1 (Verbose)
* `2` - 2 (More Verbose)
* `3` - 3 (Debug)
* `4` - 4 (Connection Debug)
* `5` - 5 (WinRM Debug)
